title: Session history persistence
why_it_matters: Codex CLI's built-in default saves local session transcripts to history.jsonl under CODEX_HOME. Sensitive content included in a transcript may remain on disk; a separate optional history.max_bytes cap can compact the oldest entries, and this rule does not inspect that cap or every configuration layer that can override the user-level setting.

outcomes.message: The inspected user-level config sets history.persistence = "none".

outcomes.when: history.persistence is explicitly "save-all" in the user-level config
outcomes.message: The inspected user-level config explicitly enables local history persistence (history.persistence = "save-all").

outcomes.when: history.persistence is unset in the user-level config or the user-level config is absent
outcomes.message: Effective history persistence cannot be determined from an unset user-level value because uninspected configuration layers may supply or override it.
outcomes.unknown_reason: history.persistence is unset in the user-level config; uninspected system, profile, trusted-project, or CLI layers may determine the effective value.

outcomes.when: history.persistence is set to a value outside the documented enum
outcomes.message: history.persistence is set to an unrecognized value — raw values are never displayed.
outcomes.unknown_reason: Value is outside the documented enum for the tested version range; it cannot be interpreted safely.

sources.title: Codex configuration reference
sources.notes: Documents history.persistence values save-all | none, user-level and trusted-project config, and profile files. The developers.openai.com/codex/config-reference alias redirects to this canonical URL.

sources.title: Codex advanced configuration
sources.notes: States the default persistence behavior applied when the key is unset and documents optional history.max_bytes compaction. The developers.openai.com/codex/config-advanced alias redirects to this canonical URL.

sources.title: Codex configuration basics
sources.notes: Documents configuration precedence: CLI overrides, trusted-project config, selected profile, user config, system config, then built-in defaults.

limitations: Only the user-level CODEX_HOME/config.toml is inspected; system config, selected profiles, trusted-project config, and CLI overrides are not inspected.
An explicit user-level value describes that layer only and does not prove the effective value for every profile, trusted project, or invocation.
This rule does not inspect history.max_bytes; a configured cap may compact the oldest entries in history.jsonl.
This rule cannot confirm any server-side or remote retention — no local file can.
Auth method (ChatGPT sign-in vs API key) changes data-policy interpretation and is user-confirmed-or-unknown; this rule never infers it.
unknown_conditions: Config file is a symlink or non-regular file (not followed).
history.persistence is unset in the user-level config or the user-level config is absent; other configuration layers are not inspected.
history.persistence set to a value outside allowed_render (raw values are never displayed).
Config file exceeds parse bounds (size > 1 MiB or nesting depth > 32).
